title: [Bug]: CPU quota does not work for rootless user
body:
### Issue Description

podman [--cpu-quota=limit](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-update.1.html#cpu-quota-limit) does not work as expected for rootless user - podman [stats](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-stats.1.html) shows incorrect results for CPU usage ~190% where are expected results are 70%, 20% and 10%. For root user --cpu-quota works as expected.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. load image
`podman load -i /opt/appl/data/containers/images/busybox-arm64v8-1.35.0.tar`

2. run 3 containers with --cpu-quota set accordingly 70%, 20% and 10%
```
podman run -dt --name=container1 --network=none --cpu-quota=70000 docker.io/arm64v8/busybox:1.35.0 sh -c 'while true; do true; done'
podman run -dt --name=container2 --network=none --cpu-quota=20000 docker.io/arm64v8/busybox:1.35.0 sh -c 'while true; do true; done'
podman run -dt --name=container3 --network=none --cpu-quota=10000 docker.io/arm64v8/busybox:1.35.0 sh -c 'while true; do true; done'
```

3. check cpu usage for all running containers
`podman container stats -a`


### Describe the results you received

As result I see that cpu usage for all containers equal ~190%

```
[applexec@host ~]$
[applexec@host ~]$ podman container stats -a
ID            NAME        CPU %       MEM USAGE / LIMIT  MEM %       NET IO      BLOCK IO    PIDS
2568e6562fac  container2  6.75%       109.2MB / 2.075GB  5.26%       -- / --     -- / --     71

7bc395d29c3d  container1  6.75%       109.2MB / 2.075GB  5.26%       -- / --     -- / --     71
ID            NAME        CPU %       MEM USAGE / LIMIT  MEM %       NET IO      BLOCK IO    PIDS

ID            NAME        CPU %       MEM USAGE / LIMIT  MEM %       NET IO      BLOCK IO    PIDS

5f6cca20cbb6  container3  189.63%     109.2MB / 2.075GB  5.26%       -- / --     -- / --     71
7bc395d29c3d  container1  189.98%     109.2MB / 2.075GB  5.26%       -- / --     -- / --     71
ID            NAME        CPU %       MEM USAGE / LIMIT  MEM %       NET IO      BLOCK IO    PIDS

7bc395d29c3d  container1  188.40%     109.2MB / 2.075GB  5.27%       -- / --     -- / --     71
[applexec@host ~]$
```


### Describe the results you expected

I expect that cpu usage will be ~70%, ~20% and ~10% for appropriate container. Here is what I see for root user and I expect to see the same results for rootless.

```
[root@host ~]#
[root@host ~]# podman container stats -a
ID            NAME        CPU %       MEM USAGE / LIMIT  MEM %       NET IO      BLOCK IO    PIDS
0903ada9bc65  container1  4.74%       262.1kB / 2.075GB  0.01%       -- / --     -- / --     1

c07cdff990d4  container2  20.84%      254kB / 2.075GB    0.01%       -- / --     -- / --     1
[root@host ~]#
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

There are no additional environment details.

comments.body:
that is expected since the cpu controller is not enabled by default for unprivileged users.

More details here: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md#26-running-containers-with-resource-limits-fails-with-a-permissions-error

Delegate configs were added for all users and specific user - it does not solve issue I mentioned.

```
[root@host ~]# find / -iname delegate.conf

/etc/systemd/system/user@10003.service.d/delegate.conf

[root@host ~]# cat /etc/systemd/system/user@.service.d/delegate.conf
[Service]
Delegate=memory pids cpu cpuset
[root@host ~]#
[root@host ~]# cat /etc/systemd/system/user@10003.service.d/delegate.conf
[Service]
Delegate=memory pids cpu cpuset
```

@giuseppe I do not think this issue ready to be closed!
have you restarted the system or at least the user session?

> have you restarted the system or at least the user session?

@giuseppe yes, device was restarted.
what do you get if you run `systemd-run --scope --user -pCPUWeight=1 echo hi` ?
ops sorry, I've not noticed before that you are using ` cgroupManager: cgroupfs`.  rootless cgroups are supported only with the systemd backend.

> ops sorry, I've not noticed before that you are using ` cgroupManager: cgroupfs`. rootless cgroups are supported only with the systemd backend.

@giuseppe Is there documentation on that? Could you please share link with me?
you can enforce it with `podman --cgroup-manager=systemd run ...` or add it to the `containers.conf` file
@giuseppe I mean is this somewhere documented that `rootless cgroups are supported only with the systemd backend`

After setting `cgroup_manager = "systemd"` in containers.conf I am not able to run containers for both root and rootless users because of error `Error: OCI runtime error: systemd not supported: Operation not supported`. I created separate issue for this https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17196
